title: Propeller blade treatments for sound control
abstract: Sounds are generated by an aerial vehicle (101) during operation. For example, the motors and propellers (102) of an aerial vehicle (101) generate sounds during operation. Disclosed are systems, methods, and apparatus for actively adjusting the position of one or more propeller blade treatments (202) of a propeller blade (200) of an aerial vehicle (101) during operation of the aerial vehicle (101). For example, the propeller blade (200) may have one or more propeller blade treatments (202) that may be adjusted between two or more positions. Based on the position of the propeller blade treatments (202), the airflow over the propeller (102) is altered, thereby altering the sound generated by the propeller (102) when rotating. By altering the propeller blade treatments (202) on multiple propeller blades (200) of the aerial vehicle (101), the different sounds generated by the different propeller blades (200) may effectively cancel, reduce, and/or otherwise alter the total sound generated by the aerial vehicle (101).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An aerial vehicle comprising: a first motor configured to rotate a propeller such that the propeller generates a lifting force; the propeller including: a hub that is coupled to the motor so that the motor can rotate the propeller; a propeller blade extending from the hub, the propeller blade including: a leading edge; a tip; a trailing edge; and a plurality of propeller blade treatments positioned along the leading edge, wherein: a first set of the plurality of propeller blade treatments are positioned at different distances along the leading edge and extend above the leading edge; and a second set of the plurality of propeller blade treatments are positioned at different distances along the leading edge and extend below the leading edge. 2. The aerial vehicle of claim 1 , further comprising: a propeller blade treatment adjustment controller coupled to at least some of the plurality of propeller blade treatments and configured to move the at least some of the propeller blade treatments while the propeller blade is rotating. 3. The aerial vehicle of claim 2, wherein the propeller blade treatment adjustment controller includes: an actuator that extends along the propeller blade and contacts the at least some of the propeller blade treatments such that: when the actuator is in a first adjustment arm position, the at least some of the plurality of propeller blade treatments are in a first propeller blade treatment position, and 

 when the adjustment arm is in a second adjustment arm position, it causes the at least some of the plurality of propeller blade treatments to be in a second propeller blade treatment position that is different than the first propeller blade treatment position. 4. The aerial vehicle of claim 3, further comprising: a sensor configured to monitor a sound generated around the aerial vehicle; wherein: the propeller blade treatment adjustment controller moves the adjustment arm from the first adjustment arm position to the second adjustment arm position based at least in part on the sound; and the at least some of the plurality of propeller blade treatments cause the propeller blade to: generate a first sound when the at least some of the plurality of propeller blade treatments are in the first propeller blade treatment position and the propeller blade is rotating at a first speed; and generate a second sound when the at least some of the plurality of propeller blade treatments are in the second propeller blade treatment position and the propeller blade is rotating at the first speed; and the propeller blade treatment adjustment controller moves the adjustment arm from the first adjustment arm position to the second adjustment arm position when the second sound, when combined with the sound, will alter the sound. 5. A propeller blade, comprising: a hub; a tip; a surface area that extends between the hub and the tip, the surface area having an upper side, a lower side, a leading edge, and a trailing edge; and the leading edge extending from the hub to the tip and including a plurality of propeller blade treatments, each of the plurality of propeller blade treatments extending in a direction from the leading edge that includes at least one of a vertical component with respect to the surface area or a horizontal component with respect to the surface area. 6. The propeller blade of claim 5, further comprising: a second plurality of propeller blade treatments positioned on the upper side; and a second plurality of propeller blade treatments positioned on the underneath side. 7. The propeller blade of any of claims 5 or 6, further comprising: a propeller blade treatment adjustment controller configured to alter a position of a first propeller blade treatment of the plurality of propeller blade treatments from a first position to a second position, wherein the propeller blade generates a first sound when rotating and the first propeller blade treatment is in the first position and generates a second sound when rotating and the first propeller blade treatment is in the second position. 8. The propeller blade of claim 7, the propeller blade treatment adjustment controller including: a communication component configured to receive a wireless communication that indicates whether the first propeller blade treatment is to be in the first position or the second position; and an actuator in contact with the first propeller blade treatment and configured to move the first propeller blade treatment from the first position to the second position based at least in part on the indication included in the wireless communication. 9. The propeller blade of claim 8, further comprising: a power supply included in the propeller blade and configured to provide power to the actuator. 10. The propeller blade of any of claims 5, 6, 7, 8, or 9, wherein at least some of the plurality of propeller blade treatments vary in shape and size. 11. The propeller blade of any of claims 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10, wherein a first propeller blade treatment of the plurality of propeller blade treatments includes an actuator configured to move from a first position in which the first propeller blade treatment extends in a direction from the leading edge that includes a vertical component with respect to the surface area to a second position in which the first propeller blade treatment is approximately in a same plane as the surface area. 12. An aerial vehicle, comprising: a frame; a first motor coupled to the frame; a first propeller coupled to and rotatable by the first motor, the first propeller including a first plurality of propeller blade treatments positioned on the first propeller such that the first propeller generates a first sound when rotated by the first motor; a second motor coupled to the frame; and a second propeller coupled to and rotatable by the second motor, the second propeller including a second plurality of propeller blade treatments positioned on the second propeller such that the second propeller generates a second sound when rotated by the second motor, wherein the second sound causes interface with the first sound. 13. The aerial vehicle of claim 12, the second propeller further including: a propeller blade treatment adjustment controller configured to alter a position of at least some of the second plurality of propeller blade treatments while the second propeller is rotating such that the second propeller blade generates a third sound when the position of the at least some of the second plurality of propeller blade treatments is altered. 14. The aerial vehicle of any of claims 12 or 13, further comprising: a sensor configured to measure a sound generated by the aerial vehicle; wherein the second propeller further includes a propeller blade treatment adjustment controller configured to receive instructions to alter a position of at least some of the second plurality of propeller blade treatments from a first position to a second position such that the second propeller blade generates a third sound when the position of the at least some of the second plurality of propeller blade treatments is altered; and wherein the instructions are based at least in part on the sound measured by the sensor. 15. The aerial vehicle of any of claims 12, 13, or 14, wherein the second plurality of propeller blade treatments of the second propeller includes an inflatable portion such that the second propeller when rotating generates a first sound when the inflatable portion is inflated and generates a second sound when the inflatable portion is not inflated.
